items.headline: Knicks Celebrate Historic 2026 NBA Championship with Parade and City Hall Ceremony
items.summary: New York City is abuzz as Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani announced a City Hall ceremony for Thursday, June 18th, to honor the New York Knicks' 2026 NBA Championship. 600 free tickets will be distributed via a public lottery. A ticker-tape parade through the Canyon of Heroes will precede the ceremony, celebrating the Knicks' first title since 1973, clinched with a 4-1 series victory over the San Antonio Spurs. Jalen Brunson earned Finals MVP honors.

items.headline: No. 2 Pick Drama: Darryn Peterson Refuses Jazz Workout, Only Visits Wizards
items.summary: Highly-touted prospect Darryn Peterson from Kansas, a projected top pick in the upcoming NBA Draft, has reportedly only worked out for the Washington Wizards, who hold the No. 1 overall selection. Peterson is refusing to meet with the Utah Jazz, who own the No. 2 pick, creating significant intrigue just a week before the draft.

items.headline: Antetokounmpo, Young, Kessler Dominant in Trade Talk Ahead of Draft
items.summary: Trade rumors are intensifying across the league. Giannis Antetokounmpo remains a central figure, with the Miami Heat and Boston Celtics reportedly showing strong interest. Trae Young is also generating renewed interest on the trade market, while Walker Kessler is reportedly at odds with the Jazz regarding his restricted free agency status. The Denver Nuggets are also exploring trading Christian Braun.

items.headline: No Games Played: NBA Finals Concluded on June 13th
items.summary: The 2026 NBA Finals concluded on June 13th, with the New York Knicks defeating the San Antonio Spurs 4-1. There were no NBA games played on June 16, 2026.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's All-Star Breakout Season
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija had an All-Star breakout campaign in the 2025-26 season, leading the league in drives per game with an average of 19.4. He finished the season averaging 24.2 points per game, establishing himself as a key offensive engine for the Blazers. Despite his stellar performance, Avdija narrowly missed out on All-NBA team selections, partially due to a lingering back injury that impacted his play post-All-Star break.

items.headline: NBA Draft One Week Away: Mock Drafts Abound
items.summary: With the 2026 NBA Draft just one week away (June 23-24), teams are finalizing their draft boards. Mock drafts continue to dominate headlines, with prospects like AJ Dybantsa (BYU) and Darryn Peterson (Kansas) consistently projected as top selections.

items.headline: LeBron James' Future Headlines Approaching Free Agency
items.summary: Free agency officially kicks off on June 30th, and the biggest question mark remains the future of LeBron James. While a return to the Lakers is possible, he's reportedly open to other options for the first time since 2018. Additionally, Jalen Duren is expected to receive a significant new contract from the Detroit Pistons.

items.headline: Chicago Bulls Reportedly Hire Tiago Splitter as New Head Coach
items.summary: The Chicago Bulls are reportedly set to name Tiago Splitter, who previously served as the Portland Trail Blazers' interim coach, as their new head coach. Splitter is credited with helping Deni Avdija achieve an All-Star season in Portland.
